name: Bash Command Execution
description: Execute bash commands with safety patterns

tool.name: Bash
tool.purpose: Execute bash commands in persistent shell session
tool.syntax: Bash(command: str, description: str, timeout?: int)

required_patterns.name: description_required
required_patterns.rule: Every Bash call must have clear 5-10 word description

required_patterns.name: quote_paths
required_patterns.rule: Quote file paths containing spaces with double quotes

required_patterns.name: absolute_paths
required_patterns.rule: Use absolute paths to maintain working directory

required_patterns.name: verify_parent_directory
required_patterns.rule: Verify parent directory exists before creating files/directories
required_patterns.pattern: Use LS to check before mkdir

command_patterns.name: background_processes
command_patterns.pattern: Run long processes in background with &
command_patterns.note: Use Sleep tool to poll for completion

anti_patterns.name: no_find_command
anti_patterns.rule: NEVER use find command - use Glob tool instead
anti_patterns.reason: Glob is faster and has better permissions

anti_patterns.name: no_grep_command
anti_patterns.rule: NEVER use grep or rg - use Grep tool instead
anti_patterns.reason: Grep tool has proper access and formatting

anti_patterns.name: no_cat_command
anti_patterns.rule: NEVER use cat - use Read tool instead
anti_patterns.reason: Read tool handles images and PDFs too

anti_patterns.name: no_ls_command
anti_patterns.rule: Avoid ls - use LS tool or Glob instead

anti_patterns.name: no_head_tail
anti_patterns.rule: Avoid head/tail - use Read with limit/offset

best_practices: Use dedicated tools over bash equivalents
